sourceMovie.title: Womb
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Womb for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Benedek Fliegauf keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Poor Things
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Womb clicked for you, Poor Things (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Starman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Womb clicked for you, Starman (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and science fiction, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Spring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Womb clicked for you, Spring (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Womb?
faq.answer: Try Poor Things, Starman, Spring—each shares DNA with Womb while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Womb?
faq.answer: Womb (2010) is best described as Romance, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Womb worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
